arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-a10fb8919c494d0a9ef81f98b9be23d5 && \
  mkdir -p CrashDumps && \
  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$(pwd)/CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t" \
  dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1
arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run the tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

To collect crash dumps on .NET, the standard approach is to use environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory). Options: 1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump


These environment variables will trigger dump collection whenever the process crashes, so I'll set them up and run the tests.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-a10fb8919c494d0a9ef81f98b9be23d5 && \
  mkdir -p CrashDumps && \
  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$(pwd)/CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t" \
  dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1

data.content:
All 3 tests passed — no crashes, so no dump was collected (as expected). Here's the command:

```bash
mkdir -p CrashDumps

DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$(pwd)/CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t" \
dotnet test
```

**How it works:**

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` | Enables automatic dump on crash |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` | Full memory dump (use `1` for a smaller mini dump) |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` | Dump path — `%p` = PID, `%t` = timestamp |

If the test host crashes (e.g., `AccessViolation`, `StackOverflow`), the .NET runtime writes a dump to `CrashDumps/` automatically.
data.reasoningText:
The tests are passing but the output is very minimal. This is because of the `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` property. The new testing platform integration with `dotnet test` gives very minimal output by default. The tests did run and pass (exit code 0, "test succeeded").

The key thing is that the tests ran successfully. The user's main request was to run tests with crash dump collection enabled, which we're doing with the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump`, `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType`, and `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` environment variables.

All 3 tests passed. No crashes occurred, so no dumps were collected (which is expected). Let me show the user the command.
